title: Add collective offerings tab to localities with budget integration

requirements.summary: Add a 'Ofiary zbiorowe' (Collective Offerings) tab to the expanded locality card in the Miejscowości section
acceptanceCriteria: When a locality card is expanded, a new 'Ofiary zbiorowe' tab is visible alongside the residents list
The tab displays a list of collective offerings associated with that locality

file_operations.description: Add a new 'Ofiary zbiorowe' tab to the expanded locality card UI. Introduce a tab navigation system (if not present) that includes 'Mieszkańcy' and 'Ofiary zbiorowe' tabs. When the 'Ofiary zbiorowe' tab is active, display a list of collective offerings for the selected locality by calling getCollectiveOfferingsByLocality from the backend. Use the existing useGetCollectiveOfferingsByLocality hook or add it if missing. Ensure the tab state is preserved when switching between localities.

requirements.summary: Create a form in the Collective Offerings tab to add new collective offerings with amount, date, and description fields
acceptanceCriteria: Users can input an offering amount, date, and optional description
The form validates that amount is a positive number
Submission creates a new collective offering record linked to the locality

file_operations.description: Enhance the dialog to accept a locality parameter and pre-fill the locality field when opened from the Miejscowości page. Ensure the form includes amount (BigInt, validated as positive), date (timestamp), and description (Text) fields. Add validation to prevent submission of negative or zero amounts. Wire the submission to call addCollectiveOffering backend method and invalidate relevant queries (collective offerings and budget transactions).

Add a button in the 'Ofiary zbiorowe' tab to open the CollectiveOfferingDialog. Pass the selected locality name to the dialog so it pre-fills the locality field. After successful submission, refresh the collective offerings list for that locality.

Add or verify the useGetCollectiveOfferingsByLocality query hook that calls getCollectiveOfferingsByLocality(locality: string). Add or verify the useAddCollectiveOffering mutation hook that calls addCollectiveOffering and invalidates both 'collectiveOfferings' and 'budgetTransactions' query keys to ensure budget synchronization.

requirements.summary: Automatically create a budget transaction entry when a collective offering is added or edited in Miejscowości
acceptanceCriteria: When a collective offering is created, a corresponding budget transaction is automatically created with type 'income', category 'Ofiary zbiorowe', and the same amount and date
The budget transaction description includes the locality name
When a collective offering is edited, the corresponding budget transaction is updated to match
When a collective offering is deleted, the corresponding budget transaction is also deleted

file_operations.description: Ensure the useAddCollectiveOffering, useUpdateCollectiveOffering, and useDeleteCollectiveOffering mutation hooks invalidate both 'collectiveOfferings' and 'budgetTransactions' query keys after successful operations. This ensures the UI reflects the backend's automatic budget transaction creation/update/deletion. Add onSuccess callbacks to these mutations to show success toasts indicating that both the offering and budget were updated.

Update the dialog's submission logic to handle both create and edit modes. After successful mutation, display a toast message confirming that the collective offering was saved and the budget was automatically updated. Ensure the dialog accepts an optional existing offering for edit mode and pre-fills all fields accordingly.

Add edit and delete buttons next to each collective offering in the 'Ofiary zbiorowe' tab list. Wire the edit button to open the CollectiveOfferingDialog in edit mode with the selected offering data. Wire the delete button to call the useDeleteCollectiveOffering mutation with confirmation dialog. After delete, show a toast confirming both offering and budget transaction removal.

requirements.summary: Display collective offerings in the Budzet section with clear indication of their source locality
acceptanceCriteria: Budget transactions originating from collective offerings are clearly labeled with the locality name
Users can see in the budget view which transactions came from collective offerings
The budget totals correctly include collective offering amounts

file_operations.description: Modify the budget transaction table to display the relatedLocality field when present. For transactions with a relatedLocality value, show a badge or label indicating the source locality (e.g., 'Ofiary zbiorowe - [LocalityName]'). Ensure the category 'Ofiary zbiorowe' is clearly visible. Verify that the budget totals computation includes these transactions. Consider adding a filter or search option to isolate collective offering transactions.

requirements.summary: Allow editing and deleting collective offerings from the locality card with automatic budget synchronization
acceptanceCriteria: Users can edit existing collective offerings and see changes reflected in the budget
Users can delete collective offerings and the corresponding budget entry is removed
No orphaned budget transactions remain after collective offering deletion

file_operations.description: Ensure the 'Ofiary zbiorowe' tab displays edit and delete action buttons for each offering. Wire the edit button to open the CollectiveOfferingDialog in edit mode. Wire the delete button to call useDeleteCollectiveOffering with a confirmation dialog. After successful edit or delete, invalidate both collective offerings and budget transactions queries to reflect backend synchronization. Display toast messages confirming the action and budget sync.

Verify that useUpdateCollectiveOffering and useDeleteCollectiveOffering mutations invalidate the 'budgetTransactions' query key in their onSuccess callbacks. This ensures the Budzet section automatically refreshes to reflect changes made in the Miejscowości section. Add error handling with toast notifications for failed mutations.
